other_observers: none, but others were on the other side of the lake looking at the bird on the second day; I found this bird. Mark Peterson and John Drummond observed the bird on the second date along with others throughout the day.
duration: 1 hour the first day and 1 hour the second day
specific_location: Headquarters Pond at Chico Basin Ranch
description: This was a very short-legged tern with small rounded head. The bill was dark-red based with some black remaining on the distal end and was much shorter than in Forster's Tern, the common CO tern. In flight the bird appeared to me to be neckless, maybe front heavy to some degree. The wings were unicolor gray on the dorsal surface. Underneath the trailing edge of the primaries had a narrow black bar noticable at close range. The tibio-tarsus was reddish and barely visible the one time I saw it perched which alerted me to the fact it might be an ARTE. The bird had a dark cap with a tiny patch of white just behind the bill. While the bird was perched, I was unable to she the relationship between the wing length and tail length.Behaviors: Buoyant in flight. Acitvely fed by looping down and pulling up at the surface of the water to snatch some insect (midges) always faci ng the wind while feeding and then using the wind to carry it back to start the feeding again and again for over an hour that I watched it.Call: none heardPlumage: molting to adult breeding
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
